metric_value: Aberdeen's charter with IBM: (1) understand IBM's businesses; (2) identify value in growing top-line revenue, augmenting BD, delivering business-value/technology frameworks, and documenting client impact.
IBM's On Demand needs validation: what is application of On Demand in SCM/Supply Management? How do we demonstrate On Demand value to prospects?
IBM quote: 'Our strategic consulting competence and domain expertise is undervalued in the market' — addressed via thought leadership and demand generation.
IBM seeks increased market recognition for its proven value — Aberdeen proposes market-visibility programs.
Key IBM-Aberdeen alignment areas: business value first; G5000 customer overlap; research agenda synchrony; market visibility; actionable frameworks.
Issue requiring further alignment: 'Simply endorse me as great — old world pay for praise' — Aberdeen differentiates from pay-for-play analyst model.
notes: Notable candid framing of analyst-vendor relationships.

metric_value: Alignment challenge: need for detailed global research and regionalized frameworks — beyond standard US-centric analyst content.
Alignment challenge: how to have direct relationship with IBM BCS and fulfill analyst-relations obligations — a structural tension in IBM's multi-BU organization.
Aberdeen challenge to IBM: 'How much is real? How much is aspiration? Where should we start first?' — candid push for prioritization.
Aberdeen practice alignments across Strategic Consulting, Procurement, and SCM — each with Challenges, Needs, and Alignment layers.

Aberdeen suggested approach: three phases combining AOCS, Benchmark Study, Installed Base, On Demand Assessment, Private IBM Webinar, Value Assessment Workshop, DSETs, Maturity Grids, Business Modeling Tools.
Deliverables split: Public (AOCS, Benchmark Study) vs IBM-Specific (Installed Base, On Demand Assessment, Private IBM Webinar, Value Assessment Workshop).

Proposed Q1-Q2 Supply Management public research: Procurement Outsourcing Benchmark (Q1), Spend Category Management Strategies (Contract Labor Q1, Travel Q2), Strategic Sourcing in Retail (Q2), Best Practices in Contract Management (Q2).
Proposed joint workshop: 'On Demand Supply Management: Accelerating and Achieving Value in Supply Relationships' in conjunction with ISM Conference in May.
Proposed Strategic Consulting & SCM research: On Demand Business Value Roadmap; RFID Benchmark Study; RFID Tech Assessment; Next-Gen Order Fulfillment Strategies Benchmark.

IBM benefits from relationship: Quarterly Research Sponsorship; Lead and Demand Creation; Unlimited Analyst Access; Custom/Proprietary IBM-Branded Research; Customer Proof-Point Panelists in Webinar Series; Joint Workshop to Drive Spend Management Market Traction.
Aberdeen positioning: 'The Trusted Advisor to the Global 5,000 and Their Solution Providers for Technology-Driven Business Value, Insight, and Impact.'
Aberdeen offices circa 2003: Boston, Palo Alto, Ft. Collins.
